body: I've been holding NVDA since $200 and I'm not selling. The data center revenue is up 200% YoY and the AI buildout is just getting started. CUDA moat is real. Massive bull here.
AAPL crushed earnings again. EPS beat by $0.08, raised full-year guidance, services revenue hit a new record. I added more shares after the print.
AMD server market share keeps climbing every quarter. EPYC is in every hyperscaler now and Intel has no answer. Long AMD with a 2-year horizon.
MSFT Azure grew 29% this quarter. Copilot seats are ramping and enterprise AI spend is just beginning. Satya has positioned this perfectly. Adding on any dip.
META is firing on all cylinders. Ad revenue back to 20%+ growth, Reality Labs losses stabilized, Llama models giving them AI credibility. Mark finally delivered on efficiency year.
LLY has a near-monopoly on GLP-1 drugs for the next 3-5 years. The obesity market is a $100B+ opportunity and they have the manufacturing advantage. Overweight and not touching it.
PLTR government contracts are sticky and AIP is genuinely gaining enterprise traction. Revenue growth accelerating, rule of 40 hit for the first time. Cautiously bullish.
AWS reacceleration is real. AMZN margins expanding as retail becomes more efficient and cloud mix improves. This is the best risk/reward in mega-cap tech right now.
GOOGL search is not dying despite what everyone said about ChatGPT. AI Overviews are driving engagement, YouTube Shorts monetizing, and they still trade at 20x forward earnings. Cheap.
JPM net interest income is holding up better than expected. The consumer balance sheet is still healthy and Jamie Dimon is the best capital allocator in banking. Long.
TSLA margins are in freefall. Gross margin dropped to 17% and they keep cutting prices to maintain volume. Musk is distracted and competition from BYD is eating their lunch in China. Short.
INTC missed again and pushed back fab timeline by another year. AMD and NVDA are eating their data center business and the foundry pivot is burning cash. This is a melting ice cube.
Boeing is a disaster. Another 737 MAX quality issue, the CEO resigned, and they're burning through cash to keep the assembly line running. Avoid until management is cleaned up.

SBUX China same-store sales down 11% and domestic traffic is weak. The brand is losing relevance with younger customers and the union situation adds cost. Not touching this until there's a real turnaround plan.
WBA closing hundreds of stores and the pharmacy business has no moat anymore. Reimbursement rates keep getting cut and PBMs have all the leverage. This is a slow-motion liquidation.
MRNA has a COVID vaccine and that's it. Revenue is collapsing, the RSV vaccine flopped, and their oncology pipeline is a long shot. The stock is still priced like a growth company.
MPW debt is unsustainable and Steward Health is going bankrupt. When they have to cut the dividend the stock is going to flush. Sold my position last week.
NFLX price hikes are backfiring. Churn is up in every market they've reported and password sharing crackdown gave them a one-time boost that's already fading. Shorting the pop.
COIN is entirely dependent on crypto sentiment and regulators are circling. When the SEC wins this case the exchange model is broken. No business model in a down cycle.
GME has no online presence, no digital strategy, and the physical game retail market is shrinking every year. Ryan Cohen tweets memes but there's no actual business transformation happening.
Long NVDA, short INTC. NVDA has the CUDA software moat that takes years to replicate, INTC keeps missing on execution. Spread trade I've held for 18 months.
Thoughts on AMD vs NVDA for a 3-year hold? AMD is cheaper on EV/sales but NVDA has the ecosystem. Genuinely torn. AMD seems undervalued but NVDA keeps beating expectations.
What do you guys think about the market right now? Everything feels so uncertain. Rates, inflation, geopolitics. Hard to have conviction in anything.
BTC just hit 100k again. Crypto bull market is back, altcoin season incoming. Getting ready to ape into some small caps.
Is anyone else confused by how options pricing works? I don't understand why my call lost value when the stock went up.
Reminder that past performance does not guarantee future results. Always do your own due diligence before investing.
Just got my first paycheck from my new job. Time to start investing. Should I do index funds or individual stocks?
LLOY.L has been range-bound for years but the UK banking sector looks interesting with rates this high. Cheap on price to book.
[removed]
SPY puts. That's all I have to say.
